question: [File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: claim / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged attacker who is the current beneficiary of a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token call claim() repeatedly in the same block or across consecutive blocks without any new LP-fee accrual in the V4 locker, receiving non-zero ethAmount each time, under the precondition that the token has status DEX and lpLocks[token].locks contains a valid lock ID, by calling Portal.claim(token) → PORTAL_ROLLER.claim(token) → locker.collectFees() → transfer to _tokenBeneficiaries[token], violating the invariant that total BeneficiaryClaimed ethAmount across all calls must not exceed actual LP fees collected from the locker since the last claim, causing scoped impact: beneficiary overclaims LP-fee distributions beyond valid entitlement? Local proof idea: fork-test on BSC at block 108382650, deploy attacker as beneficiary, call claim() twice in same block, assert second call returns (0, 0) or assert sum of ethAmount equals locker.pendingFees() snapshot before first call.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: delegateClaim / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds no special role call delegateClaim(token) on a token whose _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to attacker-controlled address, under the precondition that PORTAL_ROLLER grants delegateClaim to any caller (not just ROLLER_ROLE or admin), by calling Portal.delegateClaim(token) → PORTAL_ROLLER.delegateClaim(token) → locker.collectFees() → transfer to _tokenBeneficiaries[token], violating the invariant that only authorized rollers or admins may trigger delegateClaim, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers LP-fee payout to attacker-controlled beneficiary without authorization? Local proof idea: unit test with attacker EOA calling delegateClaim on a DEX-status token, assert revert if access control is enforced, assert fee transfer if not.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: setTokenBeneficiary / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged token creator who originally set themselves as beneficiary call setTokenBeneficiary(token, attackerAddress) after launch without DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, under the precondition that PORTAL_ROLLER.setTokenBeneficiary() lacks a check that msg.sender == DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or current beneficiary, by calling Portal.setTokenBeneficiary(token, attacker) → PORTAL_ROLLER.setTokenBeneficiary(), violating the invariant that only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE may change _tokenBeneficiaries[token], causing scoped impact: attacker redirects all future LP-fee and beneficiary payouts to attacker-controlled address? Local proof idea: fork-test, call setTokenBeneficiary as non-admin, assert BeneficiaryChanged event emitted with attacker address or assert revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: collectV4Fees / PORTAL_V4_CL_LOCKER] Can an unprivileged attacker call collectV4Fees(token) permissionlessly on a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token whose _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to attacker-controlled address, under the precondition that collectV4Fees is documented as callable by anyone and the locker's collectAddress is set to Portal, by calling Portal.collectV4Fees(token) → PORTAL_V4_CL_LOCKER.collectV4Fees(token) → locker.collect() → Portal receives fees → distributes to beneficiary, violating the invariant that LP-fee distribution must only flow to the legitimate beneficiary and not be triggerable to front-run or sandwich a beneficiary change, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers fee collection at a moment when _tokenBeneficiaries[token] points to attacker address after a setTokenBeneficiary call? Local proof idea: fork-test sequence: setTokenBeneficiary(token, attacker), collectV4Fees(token), assert attacker receives BNB.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: collectV4Fees / TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE / TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE] Can an unprivileged attacker call collectV4Fees(token) on a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 token that has just crossed the DEX threshold but whose TaxProcessor fee rate has not yet been updated from TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE (6000) to TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE (4100), under the precondition that the fee rate transition happens inside the migrator's luanchToDEX() call and collectV4Fees is permissionless, by calling collectV4Fees before migration finalizes, violating the invariant that LP-fee distribution rate must reflect the post-migration TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE (4100 bps to protocol) not the bonding-curve rate (6000 bps), causing scoped impact: attacker triggers fee collection at wrong rate, causing protocol to receive excess 1900 bps of LP fees that should go to token holders? Local proof idea: fork-test, trigger DEX threshold crossing, call collectV4Fees before finalizeMigration(), compare fee split.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _mintToken / _circulatingSupply / PackedTokenStateV2.circulatingSupply] Can an unprivileged buyer cause Portal's stored circulatingSupply to diverge from the token's actual outstanding balance, under the precondition that TOKEN_V2_PERMIT tokens use IERC20.safeTransfer from Portal's own balance and _mintToken increments state.circulatingSupply by the raw amount before any tax deduction, by executing a buy on a TOKEN_TAXED_V2 token where the tax token's transfer hook burns or deflects tokens before they reach the recipient, violating the invariant that _packedTokenStates[token].slot1.circulatingSupply == sum of all tokens held outside Portal, causing scoped impact: inflated circulatingSupply allows attacker to sell more tokens than they hold, extracting excess BNB from the reserve? Local proof idea: unit test with deflation token, buy 1000 tokens, assert circulatingSupply stored == actual tokens received by buyer.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _burnToken / payer == address(this) optimization] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the gas-saving branch in _burnToken where if payer == address(this) the safeTransferFrom is skipped, under the precondition that an attacker can cause Portal to hold the attacker's tokens (e.g., by directly transferring tokens to Portal before a sell), by transferring tokens to Portal address then calling swapExactInput(sell) where the implementation reads payer as address(this) and skips the transferFrom, violating the invariant that _burnToken must always verify the payer actually holds the tokens being burned, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens they do not own, extracting BNB from the reserve? Local proof idea: fork-test, transfer 1000 tokens to Portal, call sell for 1000 tokens from attacker EOA, assert attacker receives BNB without having held tokens.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _eraseToken / _setTokenCirculatingSupply] Can an unprivileged attacker benefit from the fact that _eraseToken sends tokens to the dead address WITHOUT decrementing circulatingSupply, under the precondition that _eraseToken is called during migration or launch to burn excess tokens and the stored circulatingSupply remains higher than actual outstanding supply, by subsequently selling tokens against a reserve that was calculated using the inflated circulatingSupply, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply must equal actual tokens in circulation after any erase operation, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens at a price calculated from inflated supply, receiving more BNB than the curve should return? Local proof idea: unit test, call _eraseToken(token, amount), assert circulatingSupply unchanged, then compute sell price and compare with correct price.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _getTokenState / PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER / legacy parsing] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the legacy vs V2 state parsing in _getTokenState where the header byte determines which parsing path is used, under the precondition that a legacy token's packed state has its lowest byte (status) equal to 0xff (PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER), causing _getTokenState to parse it as a V2 state and read reserve/circulatingSupply from slot1 which contains garbage data, by creating a legacy token whose status byte happens to be 0xff through a specific sequence of state transitions, violating the invariant that _getTokenState must always return correct reserve and circulatingSupply regardless of token version, causing scoped impact: attacker buys/sells against a token with corrupted reserve/supply state, extracting excess BNB? Local proof idea: unit test, craft a packed state where byte0 == 0xff for a legacy token, call _getTokenState, assert returned reserve/supply are correct.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _circulatingSupply / IToken.totalSupply() for legacy tokens] Can an unprivileged attacker manipulate the circulating supply reading for TOKEN_LEGACY_MINT_NO_PERMIT tokens by exploiting that _circulatingSupply reads IToken(token).totalSupply() live rather than the stored value, under the precondition that the legacy token's totalSupply() can be inflated by minting (if the token owner is compromised) or deflated by burning, by triggering a sell after artificially reducing totalSupply() to make the curve think fewer tokens are in circulation, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply used in bonding curve calculations must reflect actual tokens sold by Portal, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens at a higher price than the curve should return? Local proof idea: unit test with mock legacy token whose totalSupply() returns attacker-controlled value, assert sell price calculation uses manipulated supply.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: estimateSupplyV2 / estimateReserveV2 / reserveDecimals scaling] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit rounding in estimateReserveV2 for a non-18 decimal quote token (e.g., 6-decimal USDC) where the scaled reserve is divided by scaleFactor with ceiling rounding, under the precondition that a token uses a 6-decimal quote token and the bonding curve reserve is near a rounding boundary, by selling a precise amount of tokens such that estimateReserveV2 rounds up the required reserve by 1 unit but the actual reserve held is exactly at the boundary, violating the invariant that Portal's quote token balance must always be >= sum of all estimated reserves for outstanding tokens, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts 1 extra unit of quote token per sell, accumulating over many sells? Local proof idea: fuzz test estimateReserveV2 with reserveDecimals=6, assert Portal.quoteBalance >= estimateReserveV2(circulatingSupply) after each sell.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: estimateSupplyV2 / scaleFactor overflow] Can an unprivileged attacker cause an overflow or underflow in estimateSupplyV2 when reserveDecimals is 0 (scaleFactor = 10^18), under the precondition that a quote token with 0 decimals is configured and the reserve amount is large enough that reserve * 10^18 overflows uint256, by calling swapExactInput with a large inputAmount for a 0-decimal quote token, violating the invariant that estimateSupplyV2 must not overflow for any valid reserve amount, causing scoped impact: attacker receives an astronomically large token amount due to overflow, draining Portal's token balance? Local proof idea: unit test estimateSupplyV2 with reserveDecimals=0 and reserve=type(uint128).max, assert no overflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _getSwapImplementation / _quoteTokenConfigurations] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the routing logic in _getSwapImplementation where isBuy is determined by checking _quoteTokenConfigurations[inputToken].enabled == 1, under the precondition that an attacker creates a token whose address happens to be registered as a quote token (enabled=1), by calling swapExactInput with inputToken=attackerToken and outputToken=address(0), causing _getSwapImplementation to treat it as a buy of native token with attackerToken as quote, violating the invariant that only legitimate quote tokens should be routable as buy-side inputs, causing scoped impact: attacker routes a fake buy through PORTAL_TRADE_V2 with wrong token direction, potentially extracting BNB? Local proof idea: unit test with a token address that is also a configured quote token, call swapExactInput, assert routing is correct.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _setTokenFeeProfile / FEE_ZERO / legacy tokens] Can an unprivileged attacker who is TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE exploit the silent no-op in _setTokenFeeProfile for legacy tokens (header != PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER), under the precondition that a legacy token's fee profile cannot be updated and remains FEE_GLOBAL_DEFAULT, by calling setFlapFeeProfile(legacyToken, FEE_ZERO) and expecting the fee to be zeroed but the function silently returns, violating the invariant that setFlapFeeProfile must either succeed or revert, causing scoped impact: if the intent was to zero fees for a legacy token but the call silently fails, subsequent trades still charge fees that should have been waived, or conversely if a FEE_ZERO profile was expected to be applied but wasn't, protocol fee accounting is wrong? Local proof idea: unit test, call setFlapFeeProfile on legacy token, assert fee profile unchanged, assert no revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / V7ParsedFees / feeConfigs[4] sum validation] Can an unprivileged token creator bypass the feeConfigs sum-to-10000 validation in newTokenV7 by supplying a feeConfigs array where non-NONE slots sum to exactly 10000 but include a MARKETING_OR_VAULT slot with bps=0 (which should be rejected by InvalidFeeConfigBps), under the precondition that the launcher validates sum but not individual slot bps for NONE slots, by calling newTokenV7 with feeConfigs=[{MARKETING_OR_VAULT, 0, attacker, ...}, {DEFLATION, 10000, ...}, {NONE,...}, {NONE,...}], violating the invariant that every non-NONE FeeConfig slot must have bps > 0, causing scoped impact: attacker launches token with misconfigured fee distribution, capturing all tax revenue to deflation (burn) while bypassing marketing wallet allocation? Local proof idea: unit test newTokenV7 with a MARKETING_OR_VAULT slot having bps=0, assert revert with InvalidFeeConfigBps.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / V7ParsedFees.totalMktBps / TaxProcessorV2 initialize] Can an unprivileged token creator manipulate V7ParsedFees.totalMktBps by supplying multiple MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots where the sum overflows uint16, under the precondition that totalMktBps is computed as sum of all MARKETING_OR_VAULT bps and stored as uint16, by calling newTokenV7 with four MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots each with bps=16384 (sum=65536 overflows uint16 to 0), violating the invariant that totalMktBps must equal the actual sum of all marketing bps, causing scoped impact: TaxProcessorV2 is initialized with totalMktBps=0, causing all marketing tax to be unaccounted and potentially claimable by attacker? Local proof idea: unit test V7ParsedFees parsing with four MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots summing to >65535, assert overflow detection or revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / DEFAULT_V4_NON_TAX_FEE_RATE_BPS / TOKEN_V3_PERMIT beneficiary fee] Can an unprivileged attacker who is the beneficiary of a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token launched via newTokenV7 overclaim the 1.25% bonding-curve fee (DEFAULT_V4_NON_TAX_FEE_RATE_BPS=125) by calling claim() before and after multiple buys, under the precondition that the 1.25% fee is sent to the beneficiary on each buy/sell and accumulates in the locker or Portal balance, by calling claim() after each buy to drain accumulated fees, violating the invariant that total claimed fees must equal sum of 125 bps of each trade's input amount, causing scoped impact: beneficiary overclaims if fee accounting double-counts or does not reset after each claim? Local proof idea: fork-test, buy 10 BNB worth of TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token, call claim(), assert ethAmount == 10 BNB * 125/10000.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: lockSalt / _saltLocks / SALT_LOCK_FEE] Can an unprivileged attacker lock a salt for TOKEN_V3_PERMIT and then launch a token with that salt without paying the lock fee again, under the precondition that lockSalt() stores the lock in _saltLocks[salt] and the launcher checks _saltLocks[salt].locker == msg.sender, by calling lockSalt(salt, TOKEN_V3_PERMIT) with SALT_LOCK_FEE, then calling newTokenV7 with the same salt, and the launcher marks isUsed=true but does not refund the lock fee, violating the invariant that SALT_LOCK_FEE must be forwarded to FEE_RECEIVER and not retained by Portal, causing scoped impact: if SALT_LOCK_FEE is not forwarded and stays in Portal's 1361 BNB balance, attacker can lock many salts to drain Portal's balance? Local proof idea: fork-test, call lockSalt with SALT_LOCK_FEE, assert FEE_RECEIVER balance increases by SALT_LOCK_FEE.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: lockSalt / _saltLocks / SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf] Can an unprivileged attacker who already locked a salt call lockSalt() again for the same salt to pay SALT_LOCK_FEE a second time, under the precondition that the check is SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf which reverts, but if the check is missing or bypassable, by calling lockSalt(salt, TOKEN_V3_PERMIT) twice from the same address, violating the invariant that a user cannot lock the same salt twice (SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf error), causing scoped impact: if the check is missing, attacker pays double fee but more importantly the FEE_RECEIVER receives double payment from Portal's balance? Local proof idea: unit test, call lockSalt twice with same salt and same caller, assert second call reverts with SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: ISaltOwnerProvider.getSaltOwner / VAULT_PORTAL trusted caller] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a contract that implements ISaltOwnerProvider exploit the VAULT_PORTAL trusted caller mechanism, under the precondition that when msg.sender == VAULT_PORTAL the launcher calls getSaltOwner() on msg.sender to resolve the effective salt owner, by deploying a malicious contract at VAULT_PORTAL address (if VAULT_PORTAL is not set or is address(0)) that returns attacker's address from getSaltOwner(), violating the invariant that salt ownership must be verified against the actual locker address in _saltLocks, causing scoped impact: attacker bypasses salt lock enforcement and launches a token with a salt locked by another user? Local proof idea: unit test with VAULT_PORTAL=address(0), call newTokenV7 from address(0), assert getSaltOwner() is not called on zero address.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: recoverStuckDividend / RecoverStuckDividendParams.to] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds AUDITOR_ROLE call recoverStuckDividend with to=attacker and amount=0 (full balance) to drain all dividend tokens from a Dividend contract, under the precondition that AUDITOR_ROLE is granted to attacker and the Dividend contract holds accumulated dividend tokens, by calling Portal.recoverStuckDividend([{taxToken, 0, attacker}]) → PORTAL_TWEAK.recoverStuckDividend() → Dividend.emergencyWithdraw(token, 0, attacker), violating the invariant that dividend funds must only be recoverable to legitimate recipients (not arbitrary to addresses), causing scoped impact: attacker drains all dividend rewards from the Dividend contract? Local proof idea: fork-test with AUDITOR_ROLE granted to attacker, call recoverStuckDividend with to=attacker, assert attacker receives dividend tokens.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: setTokenDividendToken / TokenDividendTokenUpdated] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE call setTokenDividendToken to change the dividend token before undistributed dividends are claimed, under the precondition that the Dividend contract holds accumulated dividends in the old token and setTokenDividendToken updates both TaxProcessor and Dividend contract atomically, by calling setTokenDividendToken(taxToken, newToken) when Dividend holds 1000 oldToken, causing the Dividend contract to now track newToken while 1000 oldToken are stuck, violating the invariant that all accrued dividends must be distributable to holders before dividend token change, causing scoped impact: holders lose access to 1000 oldToken dividends, which can then be recovered via recoverStuckDividend to attacker? Local proof idea: fork-test, accumulate dividends in oldToken, call setTokenDividendToken, assert oldToken dividends are still claimable or assert recoverStuckDividend is needed.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: excludeAddressFromDividends / AUDITOR_ROLE] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds AUDITOR_ROLE call excludeAddressFromDividends to exclude a large holder from dividend eligibility, then claim those dividends themselves, under the precondition that excluding an address from dividends redistributes their share to remaining holders, by calling excludeAddressFromDividends(taxToken, largeHolder) then buying tokens to become a large holder and claiming dividends, violating the invariant that dividend exclusion must not allow excluded holder's share to be captured by attacker, causing scoped impact: attacker captures dividend rewards that should have gone to the excluded holder? Local proof idea: fork-test, exclude largest holder, assert attacker's dividend share increases proportionally.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF / newTokenV6 / dividendToken sentinel] Can an unprivileged token creator exploit the MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF sentinel (0xfEEDFEEDfeEDFEedFEEdFEEDFeEdfEEdFeEdFEEd) by launching a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps>0, under the precondition that MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF causes the dividend token to be resolved to the tax token's own address after creation, by calling newTokenV6 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps=5000, causing the dividend contract to distribute the tax token itself as dividends, violating the invariant that when dividendBps>0 the dividend token must equal the quote token (DividendTokenMustEqualQuoteToken), causing scoped impact: attacker creates a self-referential dividend loop where tax tokens are distributed as dividends, potentially draining Portal's token balance? Local proof idea: unit test newTokenV6 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps=5000, assert revert with DividendTokenMustEqualQuoteToken.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: updateTaxTokenAddresses / TAX_MANAGER_ROLE / beneficiary redirection] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds TAX_MANAGER_ROLE call updateTaxTokenAddresses to redirect the beneficiary and feeReceiver of a V1 tax token to attacker-controlled addresses, under the precondition that TAX_MANAGER_ROLE is granted to attacker and the tax token has accumulated tax in its splitter, by calling Portal.updateTaxTokenAddresses([{taxToken, attacker, attacker}]) → PORTAL_TWEAK.updateTaxTokenAddresses() → TaxSplitter.setAddresses(attacker, attacker), violating the invariant that tax token beneficiary and feeReceiver must only be changeable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, causing scoped impact: attacker redirects all future tax distributions to attacker-controlled addresses? Local proof idea: fork-test with TAX_MANAGER_ROLE granted to attacker, call updateTaxTokenAddresses, assert TaxSplitter beneficiary == attacker.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: claim / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged attacker who is the current beneficiary of a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token call claim() repeatedly in the same block or across consecutive blocks without any new LP-fee accrual in the V4 locker, receiving non-zero ethAmount each time, under the precondition that the token has status DEX and lpLocks[token].locks contains a valid lock ID, by calling Portal.claim(token) → PORTAL_ROLLER.claim(token) → locker.collectFees() → transfer to _tokenBeneficiaries[token], violating the invariant that total BeneficiaryClaimed ethAmount across all calls must not exceed actual LP fees collected from the locker since the last claim, causing scoped impact: beneficiary overclaims LP-fee distributions beyond valid entitlement? Local proof idea: fork-test on BSC at block 108382650, deploy attacker as beneficiary, call claim() twice in same block, assert second call returns (0, 0) or assert sum of ethAmount equals locker.pendingFees() snapshot before first call.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: delegateClaim / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds no special role call delegateClaim(token) on a token whose _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to attacker-controlled address, under the precondition that PORTAL_ROLLER grants delegateClaim to any caller (not just ROLLER_ROLE or admin), by calling Portal.delegateClaim(token) → PORTAL_ROLLER.delegateClaim(token) → locker.collectFees() → transfer to _tokenBeneficiaries[token], violating the invariant that only authorized rollers or admins may trigger delegateClaim, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers LP-fee payout to attacker-controlled beneficiary without authorization? Local proof idea: unit test with attacker EOA calling delegateClaim on a DEX-status token, assert revert if access control is enforced, assert fee transfer if not.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: setTokenBeneficiary / _tokenBeneficiaries] Can an unprivileged token creator who originally set themselves as beneficiary call setTokenBeneficiary(token, attackerAddress) after launch without DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, under the precondition that PORTAL_ROLLER.setTokenBeneficiary() lacks a check that msg.sender == DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or current beneficiary, by calling Portal.setTokenBeneficiary(token, attacker) → PORTAL_ROLLER.setTokenBeneficiary(), violating the invariant that only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE may change _tokenBeneficiaries[token], causing scoped impact: attacker redirects all future LP-fee and beneficiary payouts to attacker-controlled address? Local proof idea: fork-test, call setTokenBeneficiary as non-admin, assert BeneficiaryChanged event emitted with attacker address or assert revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: collectV4Fees / PORTAL_V4_CL_LOCKER] Can an unprivileged attacker call collectV4Fees(token) permissionlessly on a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token whose _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to attacker-controlled address, under the precondition that collectV4Fees is documented as callable by anyone and the locker's collectAddress is set to Portal, by calling Portal.collectV4Fees(token) → PORTAL_V4_CL_LOCKER.collectV4Fees(token) → locker.collect() → Portal receives fees → distributes to beneficiary, violating the invariant that LP-fee distribution must only flow to the legitimate beneficiary and not be triggerable to front-run or sandwich a beneficiary change, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers fee collection at a moment when _tokenBeneficiaries[token] points to attacker address after a setTokenBeneficiary call? Local proof idea: fork-test sequence: setTokenBeneficiary(token, attacker), collectV4Fees(token), assert attacker receives BNB.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: collectV4Fees / TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE / TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE] Can an unprivileged attacker call collectV4Fees(token) on a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 token that has just crossed the DEX threshold but whose TaxProcessor fee rate has not yet been updated from TAX_PROC_BONDING_CURVE_FEE_RATE (6000) to TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE (4100), under the precondition that the fee rate transition happens inside the migrator's luanchToDEX() call and collectV4Fees is permissionless, by calling collectV4Fees before migration finalizes, violating the invariant that LP-fee distribution rate must reflect the post-migration TAX_PROC_DEX_FEE_RATE (4100 bps to protocol) not the bonding-curve rate (6000 bps), causing scoped impact: attacker triggers fee collection at wrong rate, causing protocol to receive excess 1900 bps of LP fees that should go to token holders? Local proof idea: fork-test, trigger DEX threshold crossing, call collectV4Fees before finalizeMigration(), compare fee split.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _mintToken / _circulatingSupply / PackedTokenStateV2.circulatingSupply] Can an unprivileged buyer cause Portal's stored circulatingSupply to diverge from the token's actual outstanding balance, under the precondition that TOKEN_V2_PERMIT tokens use IERC20.safeTransfer from Portal's own balance and _mintToken increments state.circulatingSupply by the raw amount before any tax deduction, by executing a buy on a TOKEN_TAXED_V2 token where the tax token's transfer hook burns or deflects tokens before they reach the recipient, violating the invariant that _packedTokenStates[token].slot1.circulatingSupply == sum of all tokens held outside Portal, causing scoped impact: inflated circulatingSupply allows attacker to sell more tokens than they hold, extracting excess BNB from the reserve? Local proof idea: unit test with deflation token, buy 1000 tokens, assert circulatingSupply stored == actual tokens received by buyer.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _burnToken / payer == address(this) optimization] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the gas-saving branch in _burnToken where if payer == address(this) the safeTransferFrom is skipped, under the precondition that an attacker can cause Portal to hold the attacker's tokens (e.g., by directly transferring tokens to Portal before a sell), by transferring tokens to Portal address then calling swapExactInput(sell) where the implementation reads payer as address(this) and skips the transferFrom, violating the invariant that _burnToken must always verify the payer actually holds the tokens being burned, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens they do not own, extracting BNB from the reserve? Local proof idea: fork-test, transfer 1000 tokens to Portal, call sell for 1000 tokens from attacker EOA, assert attacker receives BNB without having held tokens.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _eraseToken / _setTokenCirculatingSupply] Can an unprivileged attacker benefit from the fact that _eraseToken sends tokens to the dead address WITHOUT decrementing circulatingSupply, under the precondition that _eraseToken is called during migration or launch to burn excess tokens and the stored circulatingSupply remains higher than actual outstanding supply, by subsequently selling tokens against a reserve that was calculated using the inflated circulatingSupply, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply must equal actual tokens in circulation after any erase operation, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens at a price calculated from inflated supply, receiving more BNB than the curve should return? Local proof idea: unit test, call _eraseToken(token, amount), assert circulatingSupply unchanged, then compute sell price and compare with correct price.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _getTokenState / PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER / legacy parsing] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the legacy vs V2 state parsing in _getTokenState where the header byte determines which parsing path is used, under the precondition that a legacy token's packed state has its lowest byte (status) equal to 0xff (PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER), causing _getTokenState to parse it as a V2 state and read reserve/circulatingSupply from slot1 which contains garbage data, by creating a legacy token whose status byte happens to be 0xff through a specific sequence of state transitions, violating the invariant that _getTokenState must always return correct reserve and circulatingSupply regardless of token version, causing scoped impact: attacker buys/sells against a token with corrupted reserve/supply state, extracting excess BNB? Local proof idea: unit test, craft a packed state where byte0 == 0xff for a legacy token, call _getTokenState, assert returned reserve/supply are correct.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _circulatingSupply / IToken.totalSupply() for legacy tokens] Can an unprivileged attacker manipulate the circulating supply reading for TOKEN_LEGACY_MINT_NO_PERMIT tokens by exploiting that _circulatingSupply reads IToken(token).totalSupply() live rather than the stored value, under the precondition that the legacy token's totalSupply() can be inflated by minting (if the token owner is compromised) or deflated by burning, by triggering a sell after artificially reducing totalSupply() to make the curve think fewer tokens are in circulation, violating the invariant that circulatingSupply used in bonding curve calculations must reflect actual tokens sold by Portal, causing scoped impact: attacker sells tokens at a higher price than the curve should return? Local proof idea: unit test with mock legacy token whose totalSupply() returns attacker-controlled value, assert sell price calculation uses manipulated supply.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: estimateSupplyV2 / estimateReserveV2 / reserveDecimals scaling] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit rounding in estimateReserveV2 for a non-18 decimal quote token (e.g., 6-decimal USDC) where the scaled reserve is divided by scaleFactor with ceiling rounding, under the precondition that a token uses a 6-decimal quote token and the bonding curve reserve is near a rounding boundary, by selling a precise amount of tokens such that estimateReserveV2 rounds up the required reserve by 1 unit but the actual reserve held is exactly at the boundary, violating the invariant that Portal's quote token balance must always be >= sum of all estimated reserves for outstanding tokens, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts 1 extra unit of quote token per sell, accumulating over many sells? Local proof idea: fuzz test estimateReserveV2 with reserveDecimals=6, assert Portal.quoteBalance >= estimateReserveV2(circulatingSupply) after each sell.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: estimateSupplyV2 / scaleFactor overflow] Can an unprivileged attacker cause an overflow or underflow in estimateSupplyV2 when reserveDecimals is 0 (scaleFactor = 10^18), under the precondition that a quote token with 0 decimals is configured and the reserve amount is large enough that reserve * 10^18 overflows uint256, by calling swapExactInput with a large inputAmount for a 0-decimal quote token, violating the invariant that estimateSupplyV2 must not overflow for any valid reserve amount, causing scoped impact: attacker receives an astronomically large token amount due to overflow, draining Portal's token balance? Local proof idea: unit test estimateSupplyV2 with reserveDecimals=0 and reserve=type(uint128).max, assert no overflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _getSwapImplementation / _quoteTokenConfigurations] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the routing logic in _getSwapImplementation where isBuy is determined by checking _quoteTokenConfigurations[inputToken].enabled == 1, under the precondition that an attacker creates a token whose address happens to be registered as a quote token (enabled=1), by calling swapExactInput with inputToken=attackerToken and outputToken=address(0), causing _getSwapImplementation to treat it as a buy of native token with attackerToken as quote, violating the invariant that only legitimate quote tokens should be routable as buy-side inputs, causing scoped impact: attacker routes a fake buy through PORTAL_TRADE_V2 with wrong token direction, potentially extracting BNB? Local proof idea: unit test with a token address that is also a configured quote token, call swapExactInput, assert routing is correct.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: _setTokenFeeProfile / FEE_ZERO / legacy tokens] Can an unprivileged attacker who is TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE exploit the silent no-op in _setTokenFeeProfile for legacy tokens (header != PACKED_TOKEN_STATE_HEADER), under the precondition that a legacy token's fee profile cannot be updated and remains FEE_GLOBAL_DEFAULT, by calling setFlapFeeProfile(legacyToken, FEE_ZERO) and expecting the fee to be zeroed but the function silently returns, violating the invariant that setFlapFeeProfile must either succeed or revert, causing scoped impact: if the intent was to zero fees for a legacy token but the call silently fails, subsequent trades still charge fees that should have been waived, or conversely if a FEE_ZERO profile was expected to be applied but wasn't, protocol fee accounting is wrong? Local proof idea: unit test, call setFlapFeeProfile on legacy token, assert fee profile unchanged, assert no revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / V7ParsedFees / feeConfigs[4] sum validation] Can an unprivileged token creator bypass the feeConfigs sum-to-10000 validation in newTokenV7 by supplying a feeConfigs array where non-NONE slots sum to exactly 10000 but include a MARKETING_OR_VAULT slot with bps=0 (which should be rejected by InvalidFeeConfigBps), under the precondition that the launcher validates sum but not individual slot bps for NONE slots, by calling newTokenV7 with feeConfigs=[{MARKETING_OR_VAULT, 0, attacker, ...}, {DEFLATION, 10000, ...}, {NONE,...}, {NONE,...}], violating the invariant that every non-NONE FeeConfig slot must have bps > 0, causing scoped impact: attacker launches token with misconfigured fee distribution, capturing all tax revenue to deflation (burn) while bypassing marketing wallet allocation? Local proof idea: unit test newTokenV7 with a MARKETING_OR_VAULT slot having bps=0, assert revert with InvalidFeeConfigBps.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / V7ParsedFees.totalMktBps / TaxProcessorV2 initialize] Can an unprivileged token creator manipulate V7ParsedFees.totalMktBps by supplying multiple MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots where the sum overflows uint16, under the precondition that totalMktBps is computed as sum of all MARKETING_OR_VAULT bps and stored as uint16, by calling newTokenV7 with four MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots each with bps=16384 (sum=65536 overflows uint16 to 0), violating the invariant that totalMktBps must equal the actual sum of all marketing bps, causing scoped impact: TaxProcessorV2 is initialized with totalMktBps=0, causing all marketing tax to be unaccounted and potentially claimable by attacker? Local proof idea: unit test V7ParsedFees parsing with four MARKETING_OR_VAULT slots summing to >65535, assert overflow detection or revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: newTokenV7 / DEFAULT_V4_NON_TAX_FEE_RATE_BPS / TOKEN_V3_PERMIT beneficiary fee] Can an unprivileged attacker who is the beneficiary of a TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token launched via newTokenV7 overclaim the 1.25% bonding-curve fee (DEFAULT_V4_NON_TAX_FEE_RATE_BPS=125) by calling claim() before and after multiple buys, under the precondition that the 1.25% fee is sent to the beneficiary on each buy/sell and accumulates in the locker or Portal balance, by calling claim() after each buy to drain accumulated fees, violating the invariant that total claimed fees must equal sum of 125 bps of each trade's input amount, causing scoped impact: beneficiary overclaims if fee accounting double-counts or does not reset after each claim? Local proof idea: fork-test, buy 10 BNB worth of TOKEN_V3_PERMIT token, call claim(), assert ethAmount == 10 BNB * 125/10000.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: lockSalt / _saltLocks / SALT_LOCK_FEE] Can an unprivileged attacker lock a salt for TOKEN_V3_PERMIT and then launch a token with that salt without paying the lock fee again, under the precondition that lockSalt() stores the lock in _saltLocks[salt] and the launcher checks _saltLocks[salt].locker == msg.sender, by calling lockSalt(salt, TOKEN_V3_PERMIT) with SALT_LOCK_FEE, then calling newTokenV7 with the same salt, and the launcher marks isUsed=true but does not refund the lock fee, violating the invariant that SALT_LOCK_FEE must be forwarded to FEE_RECEIVER and not retained by Portal, causing scoped impact: if SALT_LOCK_FEE is not forwarded and stays in Portal's 1361 BNB balance, attacker can lock many salts to drain Portal's balance? Local proof idea: fork-test, call lockSalt with SALT_LOCK_FEE, assert FEE_RECEIVER balance increases by SALT_LOCK_FEE.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: lockSalt / _saltLocks / SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf] Can an unprivileged attacker who already locked a salt call lockSalt() again for the same salt to pay SALT_LOCK_FEE a second time, under the precondition that the check is SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf which reverts, but if the check is missing or bypassable, by calling lockSalt(salt, TOKEN_V3_PERMIT) twice from the same address, violating the invariant that a user cannot lock the same salt twice (SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf error), causing scoped impact: if the check is missing, attacker pays double fee but more importantly the FEE_RECEIVER receives double payment from Portal's balance? Local proof idea: unit test, call lockSalt twice with same salt and same caller, assert second call reverts with SaltAlreadyLockedBySelf.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: ISaltOwnerProvider.getSaltOwner / VAULT_PORTAL trusted caller] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls a contract that implements ISaltOwnerProvider exploit the VAULT_PORTAL trusted caller mechanism, under the precondition that when msg.sender == VAULT_PORTAL the launcher calls getSaltOwner() on msg.sender to resolve the effective salt owner, by deploying a malicious contract at VAULT_PORTAL address (if VAULT_PORTAL is not set or is address(0)) that returns attacker's address from getSaltOwner(), violating the invariant that salt ownership must be verified against the actual locker address in _saltLocks, causing scoped impact: attacker bypasses salt lock enforcement and launches a token with a salt locked by another user? Local proof idea: unit test with VAULT_PORTAL=address(0), call newTokenV7 from address(0), assert getSaltOwner() is not called on zero address.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: recoverStuckDividend / RecoverStuckDividendParams.to] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds AUDITOR_ROLE call recoverStuckDividend with to=attacker and amount=0 (full balance) to drain all dividend tokens from a Dividend contract, under the precondition that AUDITOR_ROLE is granted to attacker and the Dividend contract holds accumulated dividend tokens, by calling Portal.recoverStuckDividend([{taxToken, 0, attacker}]) → PORTAL_TWEAK.recoverStuckDividend() → Dividend.emergencyWithdraw(token, 0, attacker), violating the invariant that dividend funds must only be recoverable to legitimate recipients (not arbitrary to addresses), causing scoped impact: attacker drains all dividend rewards from the Dividend contract? Local proof idea: fork-test with AUDITOR_ROLE granted to attacker, call recoverStuckDividend with to=attacker, assert attacker receives dividend tokens.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: setTokenDividendToken / TokenDividendTokenUpdated] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE call setTokenDividendToken to change the dividend token before undistributed dividends are claimed, under the precondition that the Dividend contract holds accumulated dividends in the old token and setTokenDividendToken updates both TaxProcessor and Dividend contract atomically, by calling setTokenDividendToken(taxToken, newToken) when Dividend holds 1000 oldToken, causing the Dividend contract to now track newToken while 1000 oldToken are stuck, violating the invariant that all accrued dividends must be distributable to holders before dividend token change, causing scoped impact: holders lose access to 1000 oldToken dividends, which can then be recovered via recoverStuckDividend to attacker? Local proof idea: fork-test, accumulate dividends in oldToken, call setTokenDividendToken, assert oldToken dividends are still claimable or assert recoverStuckDividend is needed.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: excludeAddressFromDividends / AUDITOR_ROLE] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds AUDITOR_ROLE call excludeAddressFromDividends to exclude a large holder from dividend eligibility, then claim those dividends themselves, under the precondition that excluding an address from dividends redistributes their share to remaining holders, by calling excludeAddressFromDividends(taxToken, largeHolder) then buying tokens to become a large holder and claiming dividends, violating the invariant that dividend exclusion must not allow excluded holder's share to be captured by attacker, causing scoped impact: attacker captures dividend rewards that should have gone to the excluded holder? Local proof idea: fork-test, exclude largest holder, assert attacker's dividend share increases proportionally.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF / newTokenV6 / dividendToken sentinel] Can an unprivileged token creator exploit the MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF sentinel (0xfEEDFEEDfeEDFEedFEEdFEEDFeEdfEEdFeEdFEEd) by launching a TOKEN_TAXED_V3 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps>0, under the precondition that MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF causes the dividend token to be resolved to the tax token's own address after creation, by calling newTokenV6 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps=5000, causing the dividend contract to distribute the tax token itself as dividends, violating the invariant that when dividendBps>0 the dividend token must equal the quote token (DividendTokenMustEqualQuoteToken), causing scoped impact: attacker creates a self-referential dividend loop where tax tokens are distributed as dividends, potentially draining Portal's token balance? Local proof idea: unit test newTokenV6 with dividendToken=MAGIC_DIVIDEND_SELF and dividendBps=5000, assert revert with DividendTokenMustEqualQuoteToken.

[File: 'File Name: src/PortalBase.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets an unprivileged user overclaim beneficiary payouts, dividend rewards, LP-fee distributions, tax rewards, migration proceeds, or token-launch related rewards beyond valid entitlement'] [Function: updateTaxTokenAddresses / TAX_MANAGER_ROLE / beneficiary redirection] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds TAX_MANAGER_ROLE call updateTaxTokenAddresses to redirect the beneficiary and feeReceiver of a V1 tax token to attacker-controlled addresses, under the precondition that TAX_MANAGER_ROLE is granted to attacker and the tax token has accumulated tax in its splitter, by calling Portal.updateTaxTokenAddresses([{taxToken, attacker, attacker}]) → PORTAL_TWEAK.updateTaxTokenAddresses() → TaxSplitter.setAddresses(attacker, attacker), violating the invariant that tax token beneficiary and feeReceiver must only be changeable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, causing scoped impact: attacker redirects all future tax distributions to attacker-controlled addresses? Local proof idea: fork-test with TAX_MANAGER_ROLE granted to attacker, call updateTaxTokenAddresses, assert TaxSplitter beneficiary == attacker.
